name: ADHD Evidence-Based Intervention Library
note: All messages use upward framing (what WILL help, never what went WRONG). Max 3 actions per intervention (anti-pattern #8).

distraction_spiral.acknowledgment: Looks like things are scattered right now — that's okay.
distraction_spiral.suggestion: A 2-minute reset could help you refocus. What feels right?
distraction_spiral.evidence: Iqbal & Bailey (CHI 2008): delivering at task breakpoints reduces frustration.

sustained_disengagement.trigger: behavioral_state == distracted AND streak_minutes > 20 AND distraction_ratio > 0.7
sustained_disengagement.acknowledgment: It's been a while since your last focused stretch.
sustained_disengagement.suggestion: Sometimes the hardest part is just starting. What's the smallest step you could take?
sustained_disengagement.evidence: Steel (2007): task decomposition reduces procrastination in ADHD.

hyperfocus_check.acknowledgment: You've been deeply focused for 3+ hours — that's impressive focus!
hyperfocus_check.suggestion: Quick check: is this the most important thing right now?
hyperfocus_check.evidence: Barkley (2012): time-blindness is a core ADHD deficit.

emotional_escalation.acknowledgment: Things seem intense right now. That's a valid feeling.
emotional_escalation.suggestion: Would any of these help you process what you're feeling?
emotional_escalation.evidence: Shaw et al. (2014): emotion dysregulation is distinct from ADHD symptoms but highly co-occurring.

hyperfocus_wellbeing.acknowledgment: You've been in the zone for over 4 hours — incredible focus!
hyperfocus_wellbeing.suggestion: Your body might appreciate a quick check-in.
hyperfocus_wellbeing.evidence: Prolonged sitting + dehydration compound ADHD cognitive deficits.
